feature_list.test_description: Verify the core directly supports 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Implemented configuration supporting 100 Kbps mode; observe SCL/SDA timing.
test_steps: Configure the core for 100 Kbps I2C operation.
Initiate an I2C transfer and measure SCL/SDA bus timing.
Check that the transfer runs at normal-mode speed.
expected_results: I2C transfer operates at 100 Kbps normal mode.
SCL/SDA activity matches the configured 100 Kbps mode.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can directly support 100 Kbps normal-mode I2C operation, where implemented.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Normal: 100Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core supports 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation.
feature_list.test_constraints: Correct hardware included for 400 Kbps mode; observe SCL/SDA timing.
test_steps: Configure the core for 400 Kbps I2C operation.
Initiate an I2C transfer and measure SCL/SDA bus timing.
Check that the transfer runs at fast-mode speed.
expected_results: I2C transfer operates at 400 Kbps fast mode.
SCL/SDA activity matches the configured 400 Kbps mode.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core can support 400 Kbps fast-mode I2C operation, where correct hardware is included.
feature_list.requirement_text:
The interface defines 3 transmission speeds:
- Fast: 400Kbps
Only 100Kbps and 400Kbps modes are supported directly.

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant Classic interface signal set.
test_steps: Inspect or exercise the WISHBONE interface signals during a valid bus access.
Verify the presence and use of wb_clk_i, wb_rst_i, wb_adr_i, wb_dat_i, wb_dat_o, wb_we_i, wb_stb_i, wb_cyc_i, wb_ack_o, and wb_inta_o.
Confirm the interface behaves as a WISHBONE Classic bus interface for the exercised access.
expected_results: The specified WISHBONE interface signals are exposed and used in bus transactions.
Observed access follows WISHBONE Classic interface behavior to the extent defined here.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core shall implement a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.
feature_list.requirement_text: The core features a WISHBONE RevB.3 compliant WISHBONE Classic interface.

feature_list.test_description: Verify each WISHBONE access completes in two clock cycles.
feature_list.test_constraints: Valid WISHBONE access with wb_cyc_i and wb_stb_i asserted.
test_steps: Start a valid WISHBONE access by asserting wb_cyc_i and wb_stb_i on a clock edge.
Count wb_clk_i cycles until wb_ack_o is asserted.
Repeat for read or write access.
expected_results: wb_ack_o is asserted two clock cycles after the access starts.
Each exercised WISHBONE access takes two clock cycles.
feature_list.requirement_description: Each WISHBONE access must take two clock cycles.

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset operates when configured as active high.
test_steps: Configure ARST_LVL for active-high reset.
Assert arst_i high and observe reset behavior.
Deassert arst_i and observe release from reset.
expected_results: The core resets when arst_i is high.
The core releases from reset when arst_i is deasserted.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify asynchronous reset operates when configured as active low.
test_steps: Configure ARST_LVL for active-low reset.
Assert arst_i low and observe reset behavior.
Deassert arst_i and observe release from reset.
expected_results: The core resets when arst_i is low.
The core releases from reset when arst_i is deasserted.
feature_list.requirement_description: The asynchronous reset level is configurable as active high or active low.
feature_list.requirement_text: The asynchronous reset level can be set to either active high (1’b1) or active low (1’b0)

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes SCL pad input, output, and output-enable signals.
test_steps: Inspect or stimulate the SCL external interface.
Verify presence of scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, and scl_pad_oe.
Observe that input, output, and output-enable roles are available on the SCL pad interface.
expected_results: scl_pad_i, scl_pad_o, and scl_pad_oe signals are exposed by the core.
The SCL pad interface provides input, output, and output-enable connectivity.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable

feature_list.test_description: Verify the core exposes SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals.
test_steps: Inspect or stimulate the SDA external interface.
Verify presence of sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe.
Observe that input, output, and output-enable roles are available on the SDA pad interface.
expected_results: sda_pad_i, sda_pad_o, and sda_pad_oe signals are exposed by the core.
The SDA pad interface provides input, output, and output-enable connectivity.
feature_list.requirement_description: The core must expose SCL and SDA pad input, output, and output-enable signals for external open-drain bus connection.
feature_list.requirement_text:
scl_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Clock line input
scl_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output
scl_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Clock line output enable
sda_pad_i | 1 | Input | Serial Data line input
sda_pad_o | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output
sda_pad_oe | 1 | Output | Serial Data line output enable
